what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML/CSS phone-portrait design mockup for a decision card, showing binary, multi-option, and decided states with tappable option cards, context text, skip/error/success UI, and gold selection styling.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono plus shared piece and phone styles.
Scopes inline CSS around .screen.decision-card and dc-* component classes.
Renders three static phone mockups: binary choice, compact multi-choice, and decided confirmation.
Uses ordinal letters, option labels, descriptions, impact text, cue text, and action badges inside each option card.
Uses gold and goldwash to mark recommended, selected, choose, and decided states while the screen background stays cool.
Includes hidden error styling, a decide-later link, disabled option styling, and a success banner for the completed state.
Loads /surface/runtime/organisms.js but this file contains no local interaction script.
standouts: Shows the same decision pattern across pending, selected, and completed states in one file.
Makes the primary action visible as a bottom choose row inside active option cards.
Treats warmth/gold as the visual marker for commitment, selection, and saved state.
conventions: Uses custom design tokens for spacing, radius, type size, colors, and shared phone chrome.
Follows a phone portrait component-gallery structure with .unit, .screen, and .role captions.
Uses semantic-looking class prefixes rather than native form controls for the decision UI.
Keeps behavior implied through static classes such as recommended, selected, disabled, and show.
concerns: Option cards are div elements with cursor:pointer, so keyboard and form semantics are not represented in this file.
The hidden error state is styled but not instantiated with .show in any rendered example.
The local markup relies on external shared CSS variables and phone layout files to render as intended.
notes: A static mobile decision-card specimen showing choice, selection, and confirmation states through card styling and gold action cues.
questions: Should the option cards become real buttons or remain static presentation markup?
Should the runtime script provide state changes for choose, decide later, and error handling?
Is the recommended state meant to preselect an option or only visually suggest it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a tappable decision-card sheet archetype in native phone portrait with cool default styling and gold action states.
apparent_themes: warm action cue on cool surface
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate how a decision prompt can move from context to choice to saved confirmation while keeping the action target obvious on a phone screen.
novelty: The active option reveals a full-width embedded choose row, making the card both the selection surface and the visible action surface.
contribution: Contributes a mobile decision-card substrate with context framing, ordinal option cards, recommended/selected/disabled/error/success states, and a color-coded commitment language that a fused system would keep as a reusable decision interaction pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A static HTML/CSS phone mockup for a decision-card component showing binary, multi-option, and decided states with gold-highlighted action and selection styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for exploring a phone-native decision interaction where context, options, action cues, and saved confirmation are presented as one compact sheet pattern.
